## Articles tagged "Artificial Intelligence" (4)

- [EU AI Act for .NET Teams: What August 2026 Actually Demands](https://daily-devops.net/posts/eu-ai-act-for-dotnet-teams-august-deadline/): EU AI Act transparency rules land 2 August 2026, high-risk got a reprieve. The practical .NET checklist for what to ship now, what to build properly.
- [Building an MCP Server in .NET Without Buying the Hype](https://daily-devops.net/posts/mcp-server-in-dotnet-anti-hype-guide/): MCP is a protocol, not magic. Build a Model Context Protocol server in .NET that survives production: real code, real threats, no hand-waving.
- [You're Shipping Bugs Faster, and Your Tests Are Helping](https://daily-devops.net/posts/tunit-ai-coding-agents/): AI coding agents make you ship faster. They make your bugs faster too. Generated tests verify what the code does, not what it should. Here's why TUnit helps.
- [AI Code Review Is a Sycophant: Why It Always Approves](https://daily-devops.net/posts/ai-code-review-is-a-sycophant/): Copilot and Claude find real bugs, but miss wrong abstractions and bad designs. Understanding that gap matters more than debating the tools.
